title: 20 Asukas treatment in movie Cringe inducing
display_title: 20 Asukas treatment in movie Cringe inducing
wikitext:
[]: 2.0 Asuka's treatment in movie.. Cringe inducing? Post by Bender » Tue Aug 23, 2011 6:17 am At Asuka's treatment in Rebuild of Evangelion 2.0? I mean her character lacks any real substance other then being a demonstrator of Rei's character development not to mention there's jack mentioned on her background. We have the cash rolling Mari (*cough* Mary Sue*cough*). Her back seat to Mari and Rei's celebratory dinner part feels like one nasty ship tease ride for fans and neglecting the psychological threads that grabbed my attention in the TV series/EoE. Also her maiming by Shinji's hand really grinds my gears. Then there's the .... *facepalm* Rainbow that forms afterwards. I mean really? http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-XVHGWYY ... re=related

[]: Post by BiQ » Tue Aug 23, 2011 6:27 am Rest assured, you are not alone with that opinion on this forum. This exact facet of 2.0 has been ... rather extensively discussed during the last two years on this forum. Just check just about any thread with "Asuka" in its title in this subforum.

[]: Post by Reichu » Tue Aug 23, 2011 6:36 am BiQ wrote: Just check just about any thread with "Asuka" in its title in this subforum.

[]: Re: 2.0 Asuka's treatment in movie.. Cringe inducing? Post by SaltyJoe » Tue Aug 23, 2011 6:38 am Bender wrote: Then there's the .... *facepalm* Rainbow that forms afterwards. I mean really?

[]: Re: 2.0 Asuka's treatment in movie.. Cringe inducing? Post by Bender » Tue Aug 23, 2011 6:44 am SaltyJoe wrote: For the record, every time an Angel is killed (in this case, Bardiel) a rainbow appears. I guess they were going for a bit of dissonant imagery in that shot by accentuating it. And yeah, it's pretty crummy, but whatever, i can only guess Anno is still mad at that one foreign exchange student who refused to have sex with him.[/joke]

[]: Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Aug 23, 2011 9:30 am I fully expect in X amount of time when 3.0 is released the fans will all change their tune when Asuka rises up & redeems herself with an epic battle & female balls made of steel. She was a victim in 2.0. She won't be in 3.0. It's called storytelling. P.S. Touji was quickly dropped in NGE as well. It's Shinji not handling the situation well. P.S.S. The only thing cringe worthy about Asuka in 2.0 was the random perv shot looking straight up at her crotch when she was in bed. P.S.S.S. I got nothing.

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Aug 23, 2011 12:06 pm +1 to what Gendo'sPapa said. That crotch shot is the only part where I was caught off guard in a negative fashion when I first saw it. Repeat viewings make one almost used to it, though. There's really not much there working for it other than "Oh... The underneath of her underwear." Though it also might set up how awkward Shinji felt in the following scene (simply because the shot itself is really awkward).

[]: Post by Trajan » Tue Aug 23, 2011 3:41 pm What is it with Rebuild and crotch shots anyway?

[]: Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Aug 23, 2011 3:51 pm Trajan wrote: What is it with Rebuild and crotch shots anyway?

[]: Post by Trajan » Tue Aug 23, 2011 3:53 pm There's a difference between fan-service and exploitation and Rebuild comes dangerously close to the later IMO.

[]: Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Aug 23, 2011 3:58 pm Trajan wrote: There's a difference between fan-service and exploitation and Rebuild comes dangerously close to the later IMO.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Aug 23, 2011 4:00 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: It's called fanservice , the thing that Misato kept lying about for half NGE.

[]: Creepy decal... B/ Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Aug 23, 2011 4:28 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: She was a victim in 2.0. She won't be in 3.0. It's called storytelling.

[]: Post by chaosakita » Tue Aug 23, 2011 8:12 pm I frequently find poor treatment of animated characters cringe-inducing too. The cruelty! Especially when the story they're written in hasn't been halfway completed yet.

[]: Re: 2.0 Asuka's treatment in movie.. Cringe inducing? Post by Eva 04 » Tue Aug 23, 2011 8:49 pm Bender wrote: At Asuka's treatment in Rebuild of Evangelion 2.0? I mean her character lacks any real substance other then being a demonstrator of Rei's character development not to mention there's jack mentioned on her background. We have the cash rolling Mari (*cough* Mary Sue*cough*). Her back seat to Mari and Rei's celebratory dinner part feels like one nasty ship tease ride for fans and neglecting the psychological threads that grabbed my attention in the TV series/EoE. Also her maiming by Shinji's hand really grinds my gears. Then there's the .... *facepalm* Rainbow that forms afterwards. I mean really? http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-XVHGWYYCvE&feature=related

[]: Post by JoeD80 » Tue Aug 23, 2011 8:55 pm Trajan wrote: What is it with Rebuild and crotch shots anyway?

[]: We like short-shorts! B) Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Aug 23, 2011 10:36 pm JoeD80 wrote: Apparently girls have crotches. Who knew.

[]: Post by Brainman » Tue Aug 23, 2011 10:53 pm *reads title* Oh boy! Yeah, More! More! Yeah obviously there's something about this movie isn't there? Two years and the redness and swelling hasn't gone down (mine hasn't at least). Honestly, you certainly don't see any Rei or Misatofriends rolling around like this after two years. But what else can we do in the stretch? For the record, the way I think about it now, they didn't mean for it to come across the way it did. I really think it was supposed to be poignant. Also it's obviously set-up for the resurrection. BUT. They dropped the ball in some areas. In my judgement it's because they cut too much out of her early characterization. As someone pointed out in this very thread, in the original, once you get to episode 8 Asuka just starts mauling screen time. Poor Rei has to take a back seat for a good while, despite the big smile scene in episode 6. But here she makes her big entrance then kind of slinks around in Rei's shadow for the rest of the movie. And she doesn't emerge really. The problem with this, especially for asukafriends, is that once she's out of the picture there's still a good half hour to go. And in that time frame she just evaporates from the picture. As in, the evidence of her presence is no longer observable. She's just done. Maybe if there had been more interaction between her and Shinji earlier this absence wouldn't be as jarring. It would also have given more dimension to Shinji's reaction in the Unit 03 scene (the way it stands the nature of their relationship is pretty vague at the point where he has to bite her in half). Yeah, ok, so maybe 3.0 will be an Asuka show. Maybe. Would kind of like to see that. The issue I feel is that 2.0 just bites off too much. You got Asuka's entrance, Rei's opening up, Shinji and Gendo, Bardiel stuff, Zeruel stuff, and someone named Mari too. So it just kind of happened. We (Asuka fans) just didn't get the good hand. This time. Now, as far as making objective judgements on the movie itself...yeah, there's threads doing that already. People seem to like it. But for me, just speaking personally, reduced Asuka focus and a callous dismissal is a deal breaker. Sorry. That's just how it is. So for me I guess the thing is to look for 3.0. Because the only way it makes any sense to me is if her trial of the flesh is the catalyst for a transformation into Super Asuka. And if that's how it happens, great. Good show. But as far as actually watching 2.0...I'm just too distracted by it. Edit: The movie is also unafraid of adding a lot of insult to injury it seems. Asuka gets humiliated in an elevator, gives up on romance, gets objectified, then gets raped and eaten. Mari hopping around in Unit 02 was just more sand in the face at that point.

[]: Post by Reichu » Wed Aug 24, 2011 12:22 am Brainman wrote: [Asuka] gets raped and eaten

[]: Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Aug 24, 2011 12:24 am Reichu wrote: I.e.: Why so overdramatic?
